explanation: The statement 'When a prop_0 the prop_1' is interpreted as: whenever prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must also hold (perhaps at the same time). This is an implication that, globally, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must be true as well.
The phrase 'When a prop_0 the prop_1' suggests that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time. This is a standard implication where prop_0 globally (always) implies prop_1.

This specification indicates that whenever 'prop_0' holds, 'prop_1' should hold at the same time (or immediately after, as interpreted by the implication globally). Thus, globally, if 'prop_0' is true, then 'prop_1' must also be true.

The phrase 'When a prop_0 the prop_1' is interpreted as 'Whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 should also hold at the same time.' This corresponds to the temporal logic statement that globally, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must also be true.

The statement says that whenever prop_0 holds (is true), then prop_1 should also hold (at the same time). In LTL, this is typically encoded so that globally, whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must be true as well: G (prop_0 -> prop_1).
The statement requires that whenever prop_0 holds, prop_1 must also hold at the same time. This translates to 'globally, if prop_0 holds, then prop_1 also holds'.

The phrase 'When a prop_0 the prop_1' can be interpreted as 'whenever prop_0 occurs, prop_1 occurs (perhaps at the same time or immediately after)'. In LTL, this is captured using the implication globally: G (prop_0 -> prop_1).
The statement says that whenever prop_0 holds, then prop_1 must also hold at the same instant. In LTL, this is expressed as 'globally, if prop_0 then prop_1'.

The statement suggests that whenever 'prop_0' becomes true, then 'prop_1' should happen (possibly immediately or after). In LTL, this is typically represented as 'Globally, if prop_0 then eventually prop_1', i.e., each time prop_0 occurs, prop_1 will follow at some point in the future.

The phrase "when prop_0 prop_1" is ambiguous and incomplete, but it could be interpreted as specifying that whenever prop_0 holds, then prop_1 should also hold at the same time (an implication at each moment). In LTL, this can be specified as 'globally, if prop_0 then prop_1'.
